name: The Back-Alley Duelist
concept: You are the best blade in a bad quarter, and everyone knows it — which is exactly the problem. Your reputation feeds you, shields the band, and draws every bravo who wants to be the one who put you down. You have turned a few square feet of foggy lane into a place men do not draw on your friends.
duty: Your name is the band's wall. A wall is only as good as the last man who tested it and fell.

look.label: Dress
options: whatever's clean enough to be seen in

look.label: Mark
options: a duelist's guild-token you have no right to
a reputation and nothing to show for it

gear.label: Token
options: a duelist's code you actually keep
a debt of honor owed to you

moves.name: Cross Blades
moves.trigger: When you fight one or a few with the blade, up close and personal.

results.success: You have the better of them; press and they fall or flee.
results.failure: The exchange goes against you; the GM says how it costs you.
advantage: Hold 1 Flourish (spend: disarm or wound without killing / drive a foe back and seize ground / turn a blow meant for an ally / cow the onlookers with your skill).
You read your opponent's style and its one weakness.
threat: You take a hit, or your guard is left open.
The fight draws a crowd, the watch, or a name you'd rather not fight.
results.triumph: A single perfect stroke ends it, and the tale of it will spread.
results.despair: You are badly wounded, or you kill someone you should not have.

hold.name: Flourish
spend: Disarm or wound your foe instead of killing them.
Drive a foe back and take the ground or the doorway.
Turn aside a blow meant for someone beside you.
Cow the onlookers with a display of pure skill.

moves.name: This Is Our Ground
moves.trigger: On the band's own turf around the Black Cat, where your name is law:
moves.text: Lead the band's defense of its turf well and raise your Standing; let the turf be taken or your name be humbled and lower it. At high Standing, most bravos will not draw on you or yours here at all — your reputation does the fighting for you.

moves.name: Duelist's Eye
moves.trigger: When you size up a fighter or a fight before steel is drawn.

results.success: Ask the GM one: how good are they, truly? what are they hiding? what will they do first? Acting on it grants a boost die.
results.failure: You misread them, and it shows once blades are out.
advantage: You choose the ground the fight happens on.
threat: They saw you measuring them, and take offense.
results.triumph: You know exactly how to beat them before you begin.

moves.name: Riposte
moves.trigger: When you turn an enemy's attack and answer it in the same breath.

results.success: Their blow slides off and yours goes home.
results.failure: You misjudge the timing and take the hit instead.
advantage: Leave them open for an ally.
Take their weapon or their balance.
threat: Your counter commits you; you can't shift to a new foe.
results.triumph: You turn their own attack into their undoing.
results.despair: The feint was the real attack, and you fell for it.

moves.name: Swagger
moves.trigger: When you trade on your fearsome name to warn off, provoke, or awe someone:
moves.text: Take a boost die to make them back down, lose their nerve, or fight you on your terms. Naming a duel you won that they witnessed grants a second boost die — but a reputation is a debt the whole city may one day call in.

moves.name: Second's Honor
moves.trigger: When you stand as a shield for someone who cannot fight for themselves.

results.success: No blade reaches them while you stand.
results.failure: You are pressed too hard, or holding the line costs you dearly.
advantage: Draw the attackers' whole attention onto you.
threat: You hold, but you're pinned and cannot leave them to help elsewhere.
results.triumph: You make defending them look so costly the attackers give up.
results.despair: You hold, and it is nearly the end of you.

bonds: ____ has stood at my back in a fight and not run; that is rarer than gold.
____ thinks my dueling will get us all killed one day, and may be right.
I am teaching ____ the blade, and fear the reputation it will hang on them.
____ once saw me lose a fight I should have won, and has never mentioned it.
I owe ____ a life; they pulled me out of a duel I could not win.
communityTie: The Black Cat sleeps easier because bravos fear your name; ____ knows how thin that reputation wears.
When trouble comes to the turf, ____ comes to fetch you first.
You keep the quarter's peace with your blade, but ____ remembers the ones you've killed to do it.

tracks.name: Standing
tracks.note: Your name's weight on the band's turf; how much the quarter defers to you.

basic: Take a move from another playbook.
Take a move from another playbook.
Increase your maximum held Flourish by 1.
Grow your legend: raise your Standing by 1 through a duel the whole quarter will retell.
Train a move you lean on: it upgrades 2 dice to proficiency (never more than its stat).
Master a signature stroke that is yours and no one else's.
advanced: Raise one stat by 1 (max 5). Rare, and only at a true turning point.
Retire the blade: hang up your reputation and become someone new, resolving the fallout in play.
Open a school: found a Bastion (a fencing hall) where your name draws students and shields the band.
